title: Zeuxine integerrima
intro: Zeuxine integerrima, also known as the orchid root or Chinese dodder root, is a type of medicinal plant native to Asia, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Zeuxine integerrima include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Zeuxine integerrima is commonly prepared as a decoction or powder and is used in traditional herbal medicine to treat a range of health conditions, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
taxonomy: Zeuxine integerrima is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and the subfamily Orchidoideae. Within the Orchidoideae subfamily, it is part of the tribe Cranichideae. The genus Zeuxine is a small group of orchids primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. By classification, Zeuxine integerrima is also known as a terrestrial orchid.
